title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line of Arvidsson, Zacha, and Mittelstadt boasts a 35-16 goal advantage this NHL season.
The line's +19 goal differential ranks just behind the Avalanche's top line, which stands at +21 this season.
Zacha, with 51 points, Arvidsson, and Mittelstadt are key to Boston's push for an NHL playoff berth.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support pediatric cancer care at MGH.
Mahr first ran the Boston Marathon in 2014, inspired by young patients supported by MGH at Heartbreak Hill.
Pediatric cancer funding is limited; Mahr aims to raise awareness and support for the MGH Pediatric Cancer Center.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A proposal to reduce Massachusetts' income tax rate from 5% to 4% aims for the November ballot.
The tax cut proposal, from the Pioneer Institute, is estimated to reduce state revenue by $5.1 billion annually.
Gov. Maura Healey opposes the measure, citing negative impacts on state funding and economic competitiveness.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial for allegedly poisoning her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with antifreeze in Salisbury.
Prosecutors claim Church mentioned poisoning earlier and evidence links her to antifreeze found in their shared home.
Defense argues Church's comments were jokes and disputes the murder accusation, highlighting Fowler's affair and substance issues.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey is proposing legislation to lift Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban and allow crossbow use by all hunters.
The proposal aims to reduce deer overpopulation, with Massachusetts exceeding its deer density goals in many regions.
Changes include reducing bowhunting setback distances, endorsed by MassWildlife and supported by 70% of public comments.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a pristine derringer pistol in a river in Bridgewater, sealed in a seemingly old case.
The pistol discovery was shared in a YouTube video, gaining over 1,700 views and 1.2 million on Reddit.
DeMontigny contacted police after finding the pistol; his YouTube channel focuses on removing debris from waterways.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old man, Sergio Josue Castellanos, charged with murder after a fatal stabbing in Chelsea, Massachusetts.
Chelsea police found multiple stabbing victims near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue at 6:45 p.m. on Thursday.
A 25-year-old victim died during surgery; Castellanos's arraignment is scheduled for Friday in Chelsea District Court.

title: Two officers injured in struggle with knife-wielding man at Forest Hills station, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two officers injured at Forest Hills MBTA station during confrontation with a knife-wielding suspect.
The suspect threatened people and attempted to stab bus tires before being subdued by police.
Investigation launched into how an officer's weapon discharged during the struggle without causing injuries.

title: Man accused of kicking dog in Roxbury before being assaulted by group
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Man charged with animal cruelty after allegedly kicking a dog into the street in Roxbury on Wednesday.
Five unidentified individuals allegedly assaulted the man shortly after the incident near 559 Dudley St.
Biscuit, the missing dog, has not been located; public encouraged to report sightings to 617-704-7726.

title: Mass. man charged with threatening FBI director’s girlfriend
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden Welch Ruml, 26, from Cambridge, charged with threatening FBI Director Kash Patel's girlfriend via email, court records show.
Threatening email to singer Alexis Wilkins linked to article on FBI resources for her security and political views.
Ruml, pleading not guilty, faces up to five years in prison if convicted, according to U.S. Attorney's Office.
